course_description:
Study of choral pedagogy, including rehearsal techniques; study of choral literatures; peer teaching activity & performance observations.

MUSC7160 allows students with an experience of choral music to develop further understandings of choral processes and skills. It is assumed that students have at least some experience as a choral singer and preferably experience as a conductor.

Master of Music students who are keen to do this course but who lack such a background are encouraged to enroll in MUSC3160 (semester two) as a preparation to this course, and then do MUSC7160 in the subsequent semester.ﾠ

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:
restrictions: Course Coordinator approval is required to enrol in this course. Course offering may be cancelled unless a minimum of 10 students enrol.

Additional timetable information
This course has THREE timetabled components:
A two hour lecture
A one hour tutorial
course_aims: Students will develop an understaning of the contexts in which choirs function effectively, and the processes used by conductors to demonstrate effective choral leadership and develop successful ensembles. Students will develop a knowledge of choral repertoire and musical style as an aid to understanding choral music as an Artform.

learning_outcomes.description: Develop the skills necessary to train choral singers to become an effective choral ensemble.
Understand the ways different choirs function, and evaluate the various strengths of different ensembles.
Understand the key role of repertoire in developing an ensemble, and demonstrate an ability to evaluate choral repertoire suitable for varying contexts.
Perceive problems encountered in the choral ensemble and formulate creative and appropriate solutions to these problems.

assessment_details.title: Conducting Task 1
assessment_details.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Performance, Practical/ Demonstration
assessment_details.task_description: Task: Conducting Mid-semester Basic Skills This task consists of a video submission and fully prepared notated score with conductor notes that you will upload to Blackboard. This task is designed to allow you to demonstrate the acquisition and internalization of core conducting skills, and provides the foundation for the final video submission in this course. You will be assessed on Beat pattern, Flow, ictus, wrist and elbow, and changing metre. Upload a video of you conducting a recording of O Magnum Mysterium (Victoria). Score available from www.cpdl.org You are required to choose a recording suitable to the style of the work to conduct. The camera will face you and be full body length. The notated score will show your conducting preparation and understanding of gesture and style, including breathing marks, cutoffs, tempo or meter changes etc. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Choral Research Repertoire Study
assessment_details.task_description: Choral Research – Repertoire and Style Study 20% Written Task – 2000 words This assignment requires you to study two pieces from the perspective of the conductor who is preparing to rehearse and perform such music. The works are: Kyrie from Missa "Aeterna Christi Munnera" by Palestrina and "The Glory of the Lord" by Handel Write an account which provides background to performance in the Renaissance and in the Baroque. For each style it should discuss such things as: The environments/contexts in which this music was performed in the period (e.g. home, church, concerts, courts, markets etc); The type of ensemble likely to have performed this repertoire in that period (size, gender, amateur or professional etc): the genre of the respective pieces discussing the typical features of that genre (motet, anthem, mass, opera, cantata, oratorio etc); The appropriate stylistic considerations when performing this music in as authentic a way as is possible. (especially with regard to meter, word stress, phrasing, texture, articulation etc) Include a “conductor’s score” of the works clearly marked with analytical and musical annotations valuable to the conductor (including form, texture, stress, breathing etc) Your submission should be double spaced and referenced appropriately. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.task_description: Early in the course you will be divided into small groups and will be expected to regularly rehearse the two pieces listed below. One is a Renaissance work and one comes from the Baroque period. In the rehearsals you will be expected to demonstrate the differences in vocal/musical style appropriate to each style. Towards the end of semester you must record your group performing both these pieces. The video recording will be submitted via blackboard by the due date (May 22). If you are submitting a link to the recording it is your responsibility to ensure the assessment can be accessed. The Renaissance work is If Ye Love Me by Thomas Tallis The Baroque work is Sicut Era t from Magnificat in B flat by Durante Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Conducting Task 2
assessment_details.due_date: 8/06/2026 - 9/06/2026 These assessments will occur as follows: Tutorial 1: Monday June 8 Tutorial 2: Tuesday June 9
assessment_details.task_description: Students are to rehearse/conduct their tutorial group for 10 minutes, demonstrating the skills you have developed during the semester. You must submit an original annotated score and self reflection as evidence of emerging skill as a choral trainer/conductor. They will conduct from this score and then hand in for assessment at the end of their conducting assessment. Students will demonstrate their knowledge and understanding of the work, show development of choral conducting and rehearsal techniques including working on tone, pitch, diction, dynamics, pacing of rehearsal and musical understanding and response to the music reflected in your gesture. Work for conducting task 2: Three Hungarian Folk Songs - Matyas Seiber ( https://www.sheetmusicplus.com/en/product/three-hungarian-folk-songs-3159427.html ) (This work must be purchased and original copies used (and submitted). You can purchase it elsewhere but from this site a minimum of 10 copies is required, so arrange a bulk order with friends, but be sure that you have purchased this voicing - SAB and piano). Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Lecture-Discussion-Practice Each week consists of a two hour lecture including approx one hour of knowledge acquisition and approx one hour of practical repertoire development. Week 1: Introduction to the course – content and assessment. Week 2: Choral Philosophy: Contexts of Choral Activity - the value of choir; the role of choral activity; why do people sing? Week 3: The role of the conductor in creating choral sound Week 4: Expressive Conducting/Choral Warm-ups Week 5: Learning in the Choir Understanding choral tone Rehearsal Techniques to improve choral tone Week 6: Choral Music in the Renaissance Week 7: Choral Music in the Baroque Week 8: Choral Music in the Classic and Romantic periods Week 9: Project Week - No Lecture or Tutorials Week10: Contemporary Music Week 11: Australian Music: Past Life Melodies and more Week12: Rehearsal Plans and preparation; Role of accompanist Week13: Course Conclusion: Choral resources; copyright; organizational support Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13 (23 Feb - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic: Tutorial - Practice Each week in tutorials student will be required to conduct musical works in preparation for assessment and participate in class activities developing their skills as a choral conductor. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
